# Intracerebral Hemorrhage

INTRACRANIAL HEMORRHAGE

Intraparenchymal hemorrhage  Hypertension most common etiology – subcortical/brainstem  Cerebral amyloid angiopathy (cortical)  Arteriovenous malformation  Neoplasm (primary central nervous system, metastatic, leukemia)  Hematologic (sickle-cell disease, neoplasm, thrombocytopenia) Moyamoya disease  Drug use (warfarin, DOACs, amphetamines, cocaine, phenypropanolamine)  Iatrogenic (peri-procedural) Subarachnoid hemorrhage  Basilar cisterns: cerebral aneurysm until proven otherwise, differential includes trauma, other vascular malformations  Isolated high convexity: trauma, cerebral amyloid angiopathy, RCVS

ICH Volume estimation = (A x B x C)/2 A = Diameter, B = Diameter perpendicular to A C = Number of slices on CT multiplied by slice thickness in cm

ICH scores and 30-day mortality

0 points: 0% 1 point: 13% 2 points: 26% 3 points: 72% 4 points: 97% 5 points: 100% 6 points: 100%

Control BP: Goal SBP 100-140
- Use labetalol 10-20 mg IVP q1h PRN or nicardipine drip (start a 5 mg/h and titrate up as needed). If a nicardipine drip is started, ask ED resident/attending to place arterial line.  A PIV can only be used for 24h.
- The emergency room is busy.  Do not rely on the nurse or ED resident to monitor the blood pressure.  The neurology resident must alert the staff if SBP>140.

Additional guidelines and workup:
- ABCs, intubation if GCS<8
- HOB > 30°
- Reverse anticoagulation, if applicable (reversal protocol chart). Hold antithrombotics.
- Discuss admission disposition with attending/fellow. o Neurology generally admits intraparenchymal hemorrhage unless neurosurgery is planned. o Subarachnoid hemorrhages go to neurosurgery unless clearly non-aneurysmal.
- Admit to NICU using “STROKE PROGRAM: ICH ADMISSION ORDERS IP UF” order set
- On admission, place SCD for DVT prophylaxis. Re-start heparin/LMWH after 24h or stable repeat imaging.
- MRI brain (+/- contrast) unless obvious hypertensive etiology
- CTA head vs. MRA head vs. conventional angiogram (unless hypertensive) – discuss with attending/fellow
- Consider UDS, also blood cultures/TEE if concern for endocarditis

![](images/intracerebral_hemorrhage_11_img2.png)
